arts.ttl: 12 players could miss Tottenham vs Man City as Pep Guardiola makes final Erling Haaland decision
arts.sum: Erling Haaland did not play for Manchester City against Liverpool or Real Madrid because of a knee injury, but Pep Guardiola hopes to have the striker back for the Tottenham Hotspur match

arts.ttl: Djed Spence waited two years for a start at Spurs. Now he’s indispensable
arts.sum: Neither Antonio Conte nor Ange Postecoglou were convinced by Spence but he’s showing his value now By Ben McAleer for WhoScored Red Djed Redemption. Return of the Djedi. The Walking Djed. Call it what you like but, in a season of few success stories at Tottenham, Djed Spence’s emergence as a key player is the feelgood story of the campaign. He even scored his first goal in the Premier League at the weekend. “A kid who had the wildest dreams to play in the Premier League. A kid who had the wildest dreams to score in the Premier League. That dream came true. Never stop believing,” wrote Spence on Instagram after Tottenham’s 4-1 win against Ipswich at Portman Road. Given the first two years of his time at Spurs, that dream did not seem likely to come true this season. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: The Football Reporters: Should Son Heung-Min and Tottenham part ways?
arts.sum: On the latest episode of The Football Reporters podcast, Mark Ogden, Rob Dawson and James Olley discuss Son Heung-Min's future at Tottenham Hotspur.

arts.ttl: Man Utd face Sociedad, Spurs play AZ Alkmaar & Rangers meet Fenerbahce
arts.sum: Manchester United face Real Sociedad in the last 16 of the Europa League, while Tottenham play AZ Alkmaar and Rangers meet Fenerbahce.

arts.ttl: “This club will need to renew” – Manchester City player says Pep’s side have ‘difficult job’ restructuring squad
arts.sum: Manchester City star Bernardo Silva has talked about the need to restructure the Citizens squad. The 30-year-old has been speaking to DAZN in Portugal on Manchester City’s season and A Bola have relayed his comments. Manchester City have been knocked out of the Champions League by Real Madrid earlier this month. arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou finally has what he wants at Tottenham and Liam Delap summer transfer question
arts.sum: Our Spurs correspondents Alasdair Gold and Rob Guest discussed the win at Ipswich and also Wednesday evening's Manchester City game in the latest episode of their podcast Gold & Guest Talk Tottenham. Ange Postecoglou has quite a decision on his hands when it comes to his Tottenham team to take on Manchester City on Wednesday evening.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou delivers positive Dominic Solanke news and when Romero and Van de Ven will return
arts.sum: But even the players enjoy it, they’d much rather come to training and feel like they’ve had a good session rather than not go through the motions but go out there knowing they’re going to have to sort of hold things back because there’s another game in a day's time." Now we can work on our patterns, whether that’s in an attacking sense or defensive sense in kind of match-like situations and at a match tempo, not for the duration but it gives players the opportunity to practice some of the things we talk about."
